Off Calendar Aug-22-2006 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Align Technology, Inc. To Supplement Its Responses To Deft Orthoclear, Inc.'S Second Set Of Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Aug-22-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Michelle L. Davidson for moving party Orthoclear, and Lee Kisman on behalf of Align Technology.

Defendants' Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Supplement Its Responses To Defendant OrthoClear, Inc.'s Second Set Of Special Interrogatories is granted. The preliminary statement and general objections are overruled and ordered stricken. All responses must be full and complete at the time they are made. As language qualifies any response by identifying it as "including," it is incomplete and inadequate. Nos. 145, 150, and 152 are moot. Plaintiff's responses otherwise are inadequate.
There is an open question as to whether responses to interrogatories are a roadmap to conduct further discovery or the end all and be all. This court is not in a position to define with certainty the degree of minutia that is required.
The court observes that counsel need to meet and confer to find a procedure for identifying areas of technology to be compared side by side. Without such a procedure in place, there will never be a way to assure that the proper inquiry and sufficiently detailed responses are being made.
Further responses shall be made within 20 days of notice of this order.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Hearing On Plaintiff And Cross-Defendants' Motion For Entry Of A Protective Order Governing Discovery Materials
Off Calendar Apr-03-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Louisa Weix, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Michelle Davidson, appeared for all Defendants/Opposing party(except for Bao Tran)
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modifications. The court does not find good cause to include a bar on the prosecution of patents. The provisions of the Protective Order are sufficient to bar the use of any materials produced subject to the Protective Order except for the purposes of the litigation.
The court would approve a provision that allows any party to designate those portions of documents, which contains their own confidential material, which is produced by third parties. Such designations would be subject to the provisions which allow challenges to any proposed designation.
The court would approve a waiver provision which would provide that there is no waiver to any right to object to disclosing or producing any information or item, except on the grounds of confidentiality and trade secret.
There is no dispute as to the filing procedures, and the court would approve the agreed upon provisions.

MODIFICATIONS:
1) Atty's eyes only - approve the provision, including Tran, and allowing challenge to the designation.
2) Sharing limited to pending cases
3) Prosecution of patents bar stipulated as to Ortho & Align counsel, but not including Tran & his atty. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion For Entry Of A Protective Order Governing Discovery Materials;Appendix
Off Calendar Apr-03-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Louisa Weix, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Michelle Davidson, appeared for all Defendants/Opposing party(except for Bao Tran)
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modifications. Plaintiff and Cross-Defendants' Motion For Entry Of A Protective Order Governing Discovery Materials is granted in part and denied in part.
The court does not find good cause to create an Attorney's Eyes Only provision.
As to the use of discovery in other actions, the court approves provisions which would require the party proposing to share the material to give notice to the party that originally produced the material, giving that party the opportunity to object to the proposed sharing.
The court does not find good cause to make any differentiation between the employees of corporate and other entities.
As to those who would be required to sign an agreement to be bound by the protective order, the court would approve a provision that all individuals given access to information, except original authors or recipients who have been identified as recipients of the material outside of the litigation, should sign an agreement to be bound by the protective order. The employees of the court would also be exempt from that provision.
MODIFICATIONS:
1) Atty's eyes only - approve the provision, including Tran, and allowing challenge to the designation.
2) Sharing limited to pending cases
3) Prosecution of patents bar stipulated as to Ortho & Align counsel, but not including Tran & his atty. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
